projectDescription: A self-contained Python ETL library for extracting data from GitHub, SonarQube, ServiceNow, and Jira into PostgreSQL, orchestrated by Apache Airflow.
keyFeatures: Single-function entry point: run_asset(name, mode) handles the full ETL lifecycle
Atomic runs with temp-table-per-run and promotion only on success
Resumable extraction via checkpoint-based retry without re-fetching
Parallel extraction: page-parallel and entity-parallel thread pool modes
Self-managing schemas: auto-create tables, additive column migration
In-process thread-safe sliding-window rate limiting per source
Pluggable token management: GitHub App JWT, ServiceNow OAuth2, Jira Basic/PAT, SonarQube static
RestAsset declarative pattern: define a new asset in ~25 lines with no custom code
Dry run mode: extract and validate without promoting to main table
Stale-run takeover: automatic recovery from orphaned runs via heartbeat monitoring
publicApiSurface: APIAsset (base class for custom REST assets)
RestAsset (declarative base class for standard REST APIs)
TransformAsset (base class for Postgres-to-Postgres SQL transforms)
Index(columns, unique, method, where, include, name)
PaginationState(has_more, cursor, next_offset, next_page, total_pages, total_records)
RunContext(run_id, mode, asset_name, partition_key, start_date, end_date, params)
